articles.title: BNY builds “AI for everyone, everywhere” with OpenAI
articles.summary: BNY Mellon has partnered with OpenAI to develop an "AI for everyone, everywhere" platform, aiming to make artificial intelligence more accessible and inclusive. This collaboration is expected to drive innovation and democratize AI adoption across various industries. By leveraging OpenAI's technology, BNY Mellon seeks to create a more equitable and widespread use of AI solutions.

articles.title: How We Used Codex to Ship Sora for Android in 28 Days
articles.summary: The team behind Sora leveraged Codex, a cutting-edge AI-powered coding tool, to rapidly develop and ship the Android version of their app in just 28 days. This achievement demonstrates the potential of Codex to accelerate development timelines and improve productivity. By automating coding tasks, Codex enabled the team to focus on higher-level creative decisions, resulting in a swift and successful launch.

articles.title: BBVA and OpenAI collaborate to transform global banking
articles.summary: BBVA has partnered with OpenAI to revolutionize global banking by leveraging artificial intelligence and machine learning. This collaboration aims to enhance customer experiences, improve operational efficiency, and drive innovation in financial services. By integrating OpenAI's technology, BBVA seeks to stay at the forefront of digital transformation in the banking industry.

articles.title: Advancing science and math with GPT-5.2
articles.summary: Researchers have unveiled GPT-5.2, a powerful AI model that can accelerate scientific discoveries and math problem-solving. This innovative tool has the potential to revolutionize fields like physics, engineering, and computer science by automating complex calculations and generating novel solutions. With GPT-5.2, scientists can explore new ideas and theories more efficiently, leading to breakthroughs in various disciplines.

articles.title: The Walt Disney Company and OpenAI reach landmark agreement to bring beloved characters to Sora
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company has partnered with OpenAI to bring its iconic characters to the Sora platform, marking a significant collaboration between entertainment and AI. This agreement will enable the integration of Disney's beloved characters into Sora's interactive experiences, opening up new possibilities for immersive storytelling. The partnership is set to revolutionize the way fans engage with Disney's characters, leveraging OpenAI's cutting-edge technology to create innovative and interactive experiences.

articles.title: Improved Gemini audio models for powerful voice experiences
articles.summary: Google has upgraded its Gemini audio models, enabling more accurate and efficient voice recognition for a range of applications. This improvement enhances the overall voice experience, allowing for better speech-to-text capabilities and more seamless interactions with virtual assistants. The updated models are expected to drive innovation in voice-powered technologies, from smart home devices to mobile apps.

articles.title: Deepening our partnership with the UK AI Security Institute
articles.summary: A leading tech company has announced a partnership with the UK AI Security Institute to advance AI security research and development. This collaboration aims to enhance the security and resilience of AI systems, driving innovation in the field. The partnership is expected to have a significant impact on the development of more secure and trustworthy AI technologies.

articles.title: Strengthening our partnership with the UK government to support prosperity and security in the AI era
articles.summary: A major tech company has announced a strengthened partnership with the UK government to drive prosperity and security in the age of AI. This collaboration aims to harness AI's potential to boost economic growth, improve public services, and enhance national security. By working together, they seek to establish the UK as a global leader in responsible AI development and deployment.

articles.title: FACTS Benchmark Suite: Systematically evaluating the factuality of large language models
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced the FACTS Benchmark Suite, a comprehensive tool for evaluating the factuality of large language models, enabling a systematic assessment of their ability to provide accurate and reliable information. This suite aims to identify and mitigate the spread of misinformation by testing language models' performance on a wide range of factual tasks. By using FACTS, developers can refine their models to improve factuality and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Engineering more resilient crops for a warming climate
articles.summary: Scientists are developing more resilient crops to combat the impacts of climate change, using advanced engineering techniques to enhance their ability to withstand rising temperatures and extreme weather conditions. This innovation has the potential to improve global food security and reduce crop losses due to environmental stress. By creating climate-resilient crops, researchers aim to ensure a more sustainable food supply for future generations.

articles.title: New in llama.cpp: Model Management
articles.summary: The latest update to llama.cpp introduces model management, allowing users to easily switch between different AI models and fine-tune them for specific tasks. This innovation enables more flexibility and customization in AI applications, making it easier to adapt to various use cases. By streamlining model management, llama.cpp enhances the overall performance and usability of its AI capabilities.

articles.title: Codex is Open Sourcing AI models
articles.summary: Codex, a cutting-edge AI platform, is open-sourcing its models, allowing developers to access and build upon its innovative technology. This move is expected to accelerate AI adoption and innovation, enabling a wider range of applications and use cases. By making its models open-source, Codex is democratizing access to AI and empowering the development community to create new and exciting solutions.

articles.title: Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker: Cost-efficient Frontier Multimodal Performance
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker, a cost-efficient AI model that achieves frontier multimodal performance, enabling efficient processing of various data types such as text, images, and audio. This innovation has the potential to improve applications like chatbots, virtual assistants, and content generation. By reducing computational costs, Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker makes multimodal AI more accessible and scalable for widespread adoption.

articles.title: Introducing swift-huggingface: The Complete Swift Client for Hugging Face
articles.summary: The Hugging Face team has introduced swift-huggingface, a comprehensive Swift client that enables seamless integration with their popular AI model hub. This new client allows developers to easily access and utilize Hugging Face's vast library of models in their Swift-based projects, expanding the possibilities for AI-powered iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS apps. By bridging the gap between Swift and Hugging Face, developers can now build more intelligent and innovative applications.

articles.title: DeepMath: A lightweight math reasoning Agent with smolagents
articles.summary: DeepMath is a novel AI agent that leverages smolagents to enable lightweight math reasoning capabilities, making it more efficient and accessible. This innovation has the potential to revolutionize math education and problem-solving by providing a more intuitive and user-friendly interface. With DeepMath, users can expect improved math understanding and accuracy, thanks to its advanced reasoning and problem-solving abilities.

articles.title: If AI replaces workers, should it also pay taxes?
articles.summary: As AI increasingly replaces human workers, a growing debate suggests that AI systems should be subject to taxation, similar to human employees, to offset the loss of tax revenue and mitigate the impact on social welfare systems. This concept, known as "robot taxes," aims to ensure that the benefits of automation are shared by society as a whole. By taxing AI, governments could generate revenue to support workers displaced by automation and fund retraining programs.

articles.title: AI agents are starting to eat SaaS
articles.summary: AI agents are increasingly replacing traditional Software as a Service (SaaS) models, offering more personalized and automated solutions. This shift is driven by AI's ability to learn and adapt to user needs, potentially disrupting the SaaS industry. As AI agents improve, they may soon become the preferred choice for tasks currently handled by SaaS applications.

articles.title: Hashcards: A plain-text spaced repetition system
articles.summary: Hashcards is a new, open-source tool that utilizes a plain-text spaced repetition system to help users memorize information more efficiently. This innovative approach allows for seamless integration with existing note-taking systems, making it easy to review and retain knowledge. By leveraging spaced repetition, Hashcards enables users to optimize their learning process and retain information more effectively.

articles.title: Elevated errors across many models
articles.summary: A recent issue has been reported on Hacker News, where multiple AI models are experiencing elevated error rates, causing disruptions to various applications and services. The cause of the issue is still unknown, but it's sparking discussions among developers and users about the reliability and stability of AI models. This widespread glitch highlights the need for more robust testing and quality control in AI development.
